Muhammad Naseer and Ms Uzma Razzaq Khan, Advocates Amici Curiae Through this constitutional petition the petitioner has called in question the order dated 1312 2018 passed by the revisional court, whereby the revision ` petition filed by the petitioner was dismissed and the order of the trial court dated 21O72O18, whereby two applications filed by the petitioner were dismissed, was upheld 2 The brief facts of the case are that respondent Nos. 3 to 5 filed a suit for recovery of maintenance allowance against Muhammad Ashraf respondent No 6ljudgment debtor ( respondent N.6") which was decreed 0n 23.09.2011 and petition i5 pending adjudication before the executing court. During execution proceedings, the petitioner stood surety for executing respondent No 6, who had disappeared later on With the efforts of the petitioner respondent No6 W،$ produced before the executing couft and was sent to civil prison The learned executing C0UI( vide 1[8 order dlated 05.04.2017 issued a direction to the petitioner to pay the decretal amount The petitioner filed two applications, one for setting aside the order dated 05 O4 2OI7 and the second for summoning father of respondent No 6 to court t0 satisfy the decree.

Muhammad Naseer and Ms Uzma Razzaq Khan, Advocates Amici Curiae Through this constitutional petition the petitioner has called in question the order dated 1312 2018 passed by the revisional court, whereby the revision ` petition filed by the petitioner was dismissed and the order of the trial court dated 21O72O18, whereby two applications filed by the petitioner were dismissed, was upheld 2 The brief facts of the case are that respondent Nos. 3 to 5 filed a suit for recovery of maintenance allowance against Muhammad Ashraf respondent No 6ljudgment debtor ( respondent N.6") which was decreed 0n 23.09.2011 and petition i5 pending adjudication before the executing court. During execution proceedings, the petitioner stood surety for executing respondent No 6, who had disappeared later on With the efforts of the petitioner respondent No6 W،$ produced before the executing couft and was sent to civil prison The learned executing C0UI( vide 1[8 order dlated 05.04.2017 issued a direction to the petitioner to pay the decretal amount The petitioner filed two applications, one for setting aside the order dated 05 O4 2OI7 and the second for summoning father of respondent No 6 to court t0 satisfy the decree. Both the applications were disiissed vide order dated 21.07.2018. Feeling of the said order, the petitioner filed a revision petition which Was also dismissed vide order dated 13.12.2018 by learned Addl: District Judge, Shorkot Both the afore referred orders dated 21.07.2018 and 13.12.2018 are under challenge by instant petition. 3 Learned counsel for the petitioner has argued that father of respondent No.6 15 4 landlord and 15 in position to satisfy the decree passed by the trial court; that the petitioner stood surety for respondent No 6 0n1 humanitarian ground without deriving any benefit; that orders are the law and result of the impugned aggrieved passed filing against misreading and non reading 0f Inaterial available 0n1 record, therefore, are liable to be set-aside 4 Learned Law Officers and learned Amici Curiae have maintained that once having stood as surety for payment of decretal amount, the petitioner cannot be absolved from satisfying the decree without consent of the decree holder 01 substitution of surety bond with leave 0f the court merely 0n the ground that the grandfather of the minors in affluent circumstances is duty bound to maintain the minors. 5. Heard Record perused 6 The moot before this Court is that whether the petitioner, having stood a$ surety for respondent No 6, could absolve himself from his liability to pay the decretal amount 0n the grounds that he had the respondent No 6 arrested, who was sent to civil prison for one year and respondent No 7 in affluent circumstances grandfather of minors is obliged to maintain the minors, therefore, the decree be executed against his property 7.

Heard Record perused 6 The moot before this Court is that whether the petitioner, having stood a$ surety for respondent No 6, could absolve himself from his liability to pay the decretal amount 0n the grounds that he had the respondent No 6 arrested, who was sent to civil prison for one year and respondent No 7 in affluent circumstances grandfather of minors is obliged to maintain the minors, therefore, the decree be executed against his property 7. The minors obtained decree for maintenance allowance against their father respondent No.6 0n1 23.09.2011 and during execution proceedings, the being point got being petitioner 8tood 48 his surcty vide order dated 19.11.2015. Subsequently, the respondent No6 without making payment of decretal amount disappeared from the court and his warrants 0f arrest WCFC issued 0n 27.11.2015, 11.12.2015, 15.01.2016, 19.02.2016 and 19.03.2016, 0n which the court while issuing warrants of arrest of ` respondent No6 directed the decree holders t0 provide details 0f property 0f the surety petitioner. The same order was repeated 0n various dates whereafter vide order dated 30.07 2OI6 the court issued orders to decree holders to details of of the petitioner and his address so he may be property arrested for satisfaction of the decree On 29IO2OI6 the judgment debtor was arrested and produced before the court and was directed to pay the decretal amount and on his refusal, he was sent to civil Subsequently, on 05.04,2OI7 the court while observing that the respondent No 6 had bcen sent to civil Ior a of one of decretal amount directed the |٧c [0 non-payment [0 the same and when the said amount was petitioner 0ay issuecl warvants of his arrest 0n 07,07.2017, |10[ Iiled an application for recalling of the thc be order 1ا0 the that the decretal date, provide prison. year period prison paid, said putitionur amoسnt Bround recovered from respondent No.7 who 15 father 0f respondent No 6ljudgment debtor and grandfather of the minors and liable to make such payment The petitioner also filed another application for summoning the father of respondent No6 but both the said applications were dismissed by the trial court 8 First this Court will deal with the objection raised by the petitioner that the grandfather is liable to maintain minors so his surety be absolved Although the statutory law does not provide for mode of maintenance of the minors by their grandfather, the said aspect is provided by the ` Muhammadan Law which has been compiled by D.F.

Subsequently, the respondent No6 without making payment of decretal amount disappeared from the court and his warrants 0f arrest WCFC issued 0n 27.11.2015, 11.12.2015, 15.01.2016, 19.02.2016 and 19.03.2016, 0n which the court while issuing warrants of arrest of ` respondent No6 directed the decree holders t0 provide details 0f property 0f the surety petitioner. The same order was repeated 0n various dates whereafter vide order dated 30.07 2OI6 the court issued orders to decree holders to details of of the petitioner and his address so he may be property arrested for satisfaction of the decree On 29IO2OI6 the judgment debtor was arrested and produced before the court and was directed to pay the decretal amount and on his refusal, he was sent to civil Subsequently, on 05.04,2OI7 the court while observing that the respondent No 6 had bcen sent to civil Ior a of one of decretal amount directed the |٧c [0 non-payment [0 the same and when the said amount was petitioner 0ay issuecl warvants of his arrest 0n 07,07.2017, |10[ Iiled an application for recalling of the thc be order 1ا0 the that the decretal date, provide prison. year period prison paid, said putitionur amoسnt Bround recovered from respondent No.7 who 15 father 0f respondent No 6ljudgment debtor and grandfather of the minors and liable to make such payment The petitioner also filed another application for summoning the father of respondent No6 but both the said applications were dismissed by the trial court 8 First this Court will deal with the objection raised by the petitioner that the grandfather is liable to maintain minors so his surety be absolved Although the statutory law does not provide for mode of maintenance of the minors by their grandfather, the said aspect is provided by the ` Muhammadan Law which has been compiled by D.F. The fuct thut the children 4٣٤ in the custody of their mother during their infancy (S 352) does not relieve the father Jrom the obligation them But the i5 nOf bound to of muintuining they they futher Iuintuin child who 1$ cupuble 0| wuintuined out oj his OF her own property If Ihe juther 15 D0OF, und incupable 0] curning by his own luabour the mother, if she is in 20$} CIrCUIStuces, 15 bound [0 Iuintain her children us the futher would be I the juther is poOr und injirw, und the mother is also poor, the obligation to maintain the children lies on the grandfuther, provided he is in cuSy cIFCUIStunces 9 The perusal of the afore referred para makes it clear that in case the father of the minor is poor; it is the obligation of grandfather in easy circumstances t0 maintain his grandchildren and his grandchildren have a right to claim maintenance allowance from him which aspect of the matter is to be decided by the court on its own merits according to the material available before it However, the said provision which provides an independent right to the minors to claim maintenance allowance from their grandfather does not absolve either judgment debtor or the petitioner fiom making of the decretal amount to the minors in terms of the bond submitted by him before the court vide order surety dated I9 12OIS by making himself Iiable to make the in case the father of the minors does not appear bcing payment payient in the court or make the said payment Section 128 of the Contract Act, 1872 provides that the liability of surety is co-extensive with that of principal debtor unless it is otherwise provide by the contract In the present case the petitioner has submitted surety for Rs.5,00,00O/ [0 be in default of respondent No6 and while accepting such liability, the petitioner had not made the same conditional upon or subject to any claim of the minors against the grandfather, therefore, said argument cannot be pressed into service to avoid such liability Besides, the surety has no right to claim that the decretal amount be recovered from the judgment debtor or anybody else or the decree holder may be directed to assert his right any other party 0[ stranger to the proceedings before recovery of the same from the surety.

it is the obligation of grandfather in easy circumstances t0 maintain his grandchildren and his grandchildren have a right to claim maintenance allowance from him which aspect of the matter is to be decided by the court on its own merits according to the material available before it However, the said provision which provides an independent right to the minors to claim maintenance allowance from their grandfather does not absolve either judgment debtor or the petitioner fiom making of the decretal amount to the minors in terms of the bond submitted by him before the court vide order surety dated I9 12OIS by making himself Iiable to make the in case the father of the minors does not appear bcing payment payient in the court or make the said payment Section 128 of the Contract Act, 1872 provides that the liability of surety is co-extensive with that of principal debtor unless it is otherwise provide by the contract In the present case the petitioner has submitted surety for Rs.5,00,00O/ [0 be in default of respondent No6 and while accepting such liability, the petitioner had not made the same conditional upon or subject to any claim of the minors against the grandfather, therefore, said argument cannot be pressed into service to avoid such liability Besides, the surety has no right to claim that the decretal amount be recovered from the judgment debtor or anybody else or the decree holder may be directed to assert his right any other party 0[ stranger to the proceedings before recovery of the same from the surety. Reliance is placed on Rofique Hazquel Mosih ظ Bank Alfolah Ld And others (2005 SCMR 10 Another raised by the is that as he has produced respondent No 6 in court who has been sent to civil by executing court, therefore, he is not obliged to make payment of the decretal amount Suffice it t0 say that arrest of the judgment debtor does not absolve the surety from making payment of the decretal paid against 72). petitioner ground prison amount and his liability is joint and several with the judgment debtor for making such payment Reliance in this behalf is placed on Jنسd حطbo s الكgف Fسسi Cort mud olers (2OI8 MLD 1296) and سسسسلساd Aسنس vS Jسdge Foسil سd 3 others (2OI5 YLR 316). Even otherwise, although provisions of Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (CPC") have not been made applicable to the proceedings before Family Court by virtue of Section 17 of the Family Courts Act, 1964 (Act) the said courts are empowered [0 adopt any procedure to its own proceedings and for the said purpose, may adopt principles of CPC a5 well. Reliance in this behalf is placed on the judgment reported as La Col Nasir Malik vs Additional Disrict Jسlge Lalore oud others (2O16 SCMR 182I) فسسسعلسا Iabish Nneen Khoa vS Additionol Disسic لسكge عسه others (2014 SCMR 1365) and Fهrzouo Rosool ذفسه OIlgrs |5. A surety may be proceeded for enforcement of his liability as provided under Section 145 of CPC which is reproduced below: "$. 145 Eسforcعس 0f سنانطعنا 0f 5٧[ Where any person has become liable as surety regulate (2011 س against 0 for the perjorwunce of uny decree or uny purt thereof or b) for the reStitution of uny property tuken in cxecution ofu decree, or c) for the puyment of uny money or Jor the fulfillment of uny condition imposed on uny person, under un order of the Court in any Suit O٣ in uny proceedings consequent thereon, the decree or order may be executed uguinst him, t0 the Cxtent t0 which he has rendered himself personally liable, in the IUMHEr herein provided for the execution of decrees, and such person shall, for the purposes of appeal, be deemed 0 purty within the meuning of Section 47: Provided that such notice 45 the Court in case thinks sufficient has been given to the surety 11.

Dr dسسسسلسا Bashir 0thgg SCMR 1361). A surety may be proceeded for enforcement of his liability as provided under Section 145 of CPC which is reproduced below: "$. 145 Eسforcعس 0f سنانطعنا 0f 5٧[ Where any person has become liable as surety regulate (2011 س against 0 for the perjorwunce of uny decree or uny purt thereof or b) for the reStitution of uny property tuken in cxecution ofu decree, or c) for the puyment of uny money or Jor the fulfillment of uny condition imposed on uny person, under un order of the Court in any Suit O٣ in uny proceedings consequent thereon, the decree or order may be executed uguinst him, t0 the Cxtent t0 which he has rendered himself personally liable, in the IUMHEr herein provided for the execution of decrees, and such person shall, for the purposes of appeal, be deemed 0 purty within the meuning of Section 47: Provided that such notice 45 the Court in case thinks sufficient has been given to the surety 11. Perusal ٥f the record shows that when the petitioner stood as surety on 19I 2OI5, he had agreed to make payment of the decretal amount in case of failure of respondent No 6 t0 d0 $0 0r 0n account of his non- appearance Although the petitioner claims that the judgment debtor was arrested by him and 0n that account he claims that respondent No6 be directed to substitute his surety, the said claim is without any substance for the reasons that for substituting the petitioner ' $ surety there must be some person available in euch g0t the court who agrees to stand surety for payment of the decretal amount in his place and that too with the leave of the court In the present case no other person has to stand surety for payment of decretal amount in place of the petitioner Moreover, the petitioner claims that the respondent No.6 be directed to substitute some other person in his place, however, the said request could have been Inade by the judgment debtor himself with permission of the court by producing another person who had to stand as surety to the satisfaction of the court or in case the court required him to do s0. After default had occurred there was no reason either to allow substitution of surety bond submitted by the petitioner or [0 absolve him from his liability t0 pay the amount which had become due against the petitioner in view of principles laid down in Section 128 of the Contract Act, 1872 and Section 145 CPC 12. At the most, the petitioner was entitled to receive notice from the court which in the opinion of the court is sufficient for proceeding against the surety in terms of Section 145 CPC The couft provided sufficient opportunities to the petitioner before proceeding him Consequently, the petitioner cannot claim to be agreed agreed agreed against
